meaning: adj.鬼鬼祟祟的,卑躬屈膝的,低贱的n.(原义:像狗一样被吊死的人) 卑鄙(或卑贱)的人
examples: A man sat at a bar, had the saddest hangdog expression.
If one does not do anything shameful, one will never show a hangdog look.
Not even Roland Emmerich can keep the hangdog spirit of Reno on a leash.
And, in fact, part of cognitive therapy is -involves teaching people to get rid of that hangdog look.
Mr. Dudley, who looks a bit like the actor Michael Moriarty, without the hangdog foreboding, has made good use in his career of a thick skin.
